payload.oneLiner: Acetabular fractures are articular hip injuries where column pattern and congruity determine operative planning.

sourceEvidence.label: Acetabular Fracture — Learning objectives
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Learning objectives for Acetabular Fracture

objectives: Localize acetabular fracture columns and articular involvement.
Explain hip stability and congruity goals after injury.
Outline operative indications and post-traumatic arthritis risk.

extractedSignals.summary: 24 high-confidence Anki card mapping(s) for curriculum node trauma-acetabular-fractures
74 Orthobullets question mapping(s) for trauma-acetabular-fractures (metadata only — no stems/answers)

metadata.description: Dense medial femoral neck cortical buttress supporting head-neck load transfer.
Bony pelvic ring formed by ilium, ischium, pubis, and sacrum.
Posterior hip nerve at risk during proximal femur exposure and reduction maneuvers.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Proximal Femur Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite proximal femur model linking head, neck, trochanters, calcar, and hip joint.
Extracapsular metaphyseal zone between neck and subtrochanteric diaphysis.
Articular femoral head supplied largely by retinacular and circumflex vessels.
Intracapsular segment between femoral head and intertrochanteric region.
Lateral knee nerve at risk in tibial plateau and proximal fibular injuries.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Medial Femoral Circumflex Artery

metadata.description: Dominant retinacular blood supply to the femoral head; vulnerable in neck fractures.
Plantar fascia and soft-tissue envelope relevant to calcaneus fracture swelling and wound risk.
Tarsometatarsal articulation complex; instability defines Lisfranc injury.

metadata.action: Plan open reduction with column-specific approach per Judet-Letournel pattern and attending preference
metadata.trigger: Displaced acetabular fracture with intra-articular step-off or column disruption

metadata.action: Discuss acetabular ORIF with attending; document instability exam and congruity goals
metadata.trigger: Posterior wall acetabular fracture with hip instability or significant articular displacement

metadata.claim_text: Acetabular fractures are classified and managed by column-and-wall patterns with articular reduction and hip congruity goals.
Describe fracture pattern, hip stability exam, column-wall language, surgical approach plan, and post-traumatic arthritis counseling.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Lower Extremity Trauma Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite pelvis-to-foot model linking pelvic ring, knee, leg, and hindfoot structures for trauma neighborhoods.
Hip socket formed by ilium, ischium, and pubis; injured in acetabular fractures.
Proximal tibial articular surface bearing medial and lateral condylar load.
Talocalcaneal articulation governing hindfoot alignment after calcaneus and talus injuries.
Femoral shaft between proximal and distal metaphyseal regions.
Tibiotalar and subtalar articular bone with limited vascular supply.
Major knee-region artery at risk in tibial plateau, distal femur, and knee dislocation trauma.
Hindfoot bone articulating with talus at the subtalar joint.

metadata.claim_text: Posterior wall acetabular fractures can present with subtle instability and are operative problems when hip subluxation risk is present.

metadata.description: Posterior pelvic anchor articulating with ilia at the sacroiliac joints.

metadata.claim_text: Femoral head-acetabulum congruity after reduction is the central determinant of long-term hip function after acetabular fracture.

metadata.description: Lateral pelvic ilium region relevant to acetabular column and pelvic ring injuries.
Quadriceps-patella-tibial tubercle linkage enabling knee extension.
Distal femoral metaphysis and condylar region forming the proximal knee.
Medial proximal femur attachment for iliopsoas; key subtrochanteric landmark.
Central foot region including navicular, cuneiforms, and cuboid linking hindfoot to forefoot.
Lateral proximal femur with abductor insertions; intertrochanteric fixation landmark.
Degenerative hip arthritis after acetabular malunion or articular incongruity.
Sesamoid bone within the extensor mechanism at the anterior knee.
Primary knee stabilizer commonly injured with tibial plateau and knee trauma.
Posterior pelvic articulation between sacrum and ilium; key pelvic ring stability structure.
Column-and-wall acetabular fracture classification framework.
Open reduction internal fixation of displaced acetabular fracture.
Posterior knee stabilizer relevant in high-energy knee and plateau injuries.
Anterior midline pelvic articulation; disruption indicates pelvic ring instability.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Posterior Wall Acetabular Fracture

metadata.description: Posterior wall fragment pattern with hip instability risk after injury.
Ball-and-socket articulation between femoral head and acetabulum.
Intra-articular hip socket fracture with column, wall, and congruity concerns.

metadata.claim_text: Treating displaced intra-articular acetabular fractures as stable injuries without column, wall, and congruity assessment.
